entry.title: The Human Cost Of Our AI-Driven Future
entry.quote: Ranta found herself losing faith in humanity. “I saw things that I never thought possible,” she told me. “How can human beings claim to be the intelligent species after what I’ve seen?”

entry.title: X changed its terms of service to let its AI train on everyone’s posts. Now users are up in arms
entry.quote: While such broad licensing with few limitations is not uncommon for a social media platform, Alex Fink, CEO and founder of Otherweb, an AI-based news reading platform that targets misinformation, told CNN that what makes X unique is that its new terms “remove any ambiguity” in contrast to other platforms that don’t spell out their intentions.

entry.title: The 3 AI Use Cases: Gods, Interns, and Cogs.
entry.quote: Drew also considers Toys as a subcategory of Interns: things like image generators, “defined by their usage by non-experts. Toys have a high tolerance for errors because they’re not being relied on for much beyond entertainment.”

entry.title: A New Artificial Intelligence Tool for Cancer
entry.quote: Current AI systems are typically trained to perform specific tasks — such as detecting cancer presence or predicting a tumor’s genetic profile — and they tend to work only in a handful of cancer types. By contrast, the new model can perform a wide array of tasks and was tested on 19 cancer types, giving it a flexibility similar to that of large language models such as ChatGPT.

entry.title: Runnin' Down a Dream: How to Succeed and Thrive in a Career You Love
entry.quote: In September of 2018, I had the opportunity to share my thoughts on how people should think about their career journey. This is something I had been thinking about for some time, and I am quite thankful to the McCombs School at the University of Texas for giving me the opportunity to share this presentation.
